title: IMPACT: Learning Internal-Model Predictive Control for Forceful Robotic Manipulation
summary: Real-world robotic manipulation often involves forceful interactions with the environment — using tools of varying weights, transporting objects of different masses, and contact-rich tasks like table wiping. Prior learning-based approaches use imitation learning policies that output target end-effector poses tracked by low-level impedance controllers, realizing forceful interactions either implicitly through steady-state tracking errors (which generalize poorly across object weights) or explicitly via wrist force/torque or tactile sensors (which require specialized hardware and add complexity). IMPACT is a framework that decouples these forceful tasks into task-planning and internal-model-based predictive control. Across extensive simulation and real-world experiments, it achieves higher success rates and improved generalization to unseen object weights, as well as better safety and energy efficiency.
